title: Roadkill

jokes.text: Who voluntarily goes to a tax seminar? / Maybe the kinds of people who want to keep their businesses?
jokes.setup: David's incredulous question about tax seminars
jokes.punchline: Alexis's practical reality check
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling dynamic - David's privilege vs Alexis's surprising business sense

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Tax seminars? You're going to tax seminars?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I have to learn about taxes, David. I can't just ignore them forever.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Why not? I have been for years.

jokes.text: This is about those stupid Tamagotchi? / Actually it is.
jokes.setup: Alexis dismissively mentioning Tamagotchi
jokes.punchline: David's earnest confirmation that yes, it really is about that
jokes.explanation: Absurd that adult siblings are fighting about digital pets, but perfectly fits their dynamic

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: A Tamagotchi? Really?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, Alexis. It's literally just a small digital pet that you have to keep alive by feeding it and taking care of it.

jokes.text: Taking care of that many is like a full-time job! / I left you with six adult Tamagotchis in perfect health!
jokes.setup: Alexis defending herself about the Tamagotchis
jokes.punchline: David's increasingly specific and ridiculous details
jokes.explanation: The specificity of 'adult Tamagotchis in perfect health' is absurdly funny and perfectly David

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I was just trying to keep them alive!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You had seventeen of them, Alexis. Seventeen. And you named them all variations of your own name.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That's not true.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Alexis, Alexiss with two S's, Lex, Lexi, Lexus — which, by the way, is a car — Lex-Anne with a hyphen, A-Lex, X-Lex, and then you just gave up and named one 'Alexis But Make It Fashion.'

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That last one was ironic.

jokes.text: You have to like actively murder them in order for that to happen!
jokes.setup: The Tamagotchi death discussion
jokes.punchline: David accusing Alexis of 'actively murdering' digital pets
jokes.explanation: The dramatic escalation to 'actively murder' about toy care is peak David hyperbole

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You're actively murdering these digital pets.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm not murdering them, David. They're not real.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: They may not be real, but your negligence is very real.

jokes.text: Last night she texted me at 3 A.M. and all it said was 'leggings.'
jokes.setup: Stevie explaining why she needs a break from Moira
jokes.punchline: The absurdly minimal but somehow perfectly Moira text
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Moira's eccentric communication style in just one word. Economy and character integration are flawless

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I need a break from Moira. She texted me 'k' this morning.

jokes.text: Unlikely... murderer.
jokes.setup: Alexis saying David might stop talking about Tamagotchis
jokes.punchline: David's deadpan 'murderer' callback
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback timing and David's inability to let things go

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not going to stop talking about Tamagotchis. I didn't murder someone.

jokes.text: ballet intensive, perhaps some mittens to curb the nail biting
jokes.setup: Moira discussing Stevie's potential
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and condescending suggestions
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Moira - patronizing advice delivered with theatrical authority

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Stevie, darling, I've been thinking about your future, and I have some thoughts. Perhaps you could be a car rental associate? You have the right amount of... availability. Or maybe a phlebotomist? You're so good with people, but not in a way that requires conversation. Oh! A parking enforcement officer. You have that natural authority that comes from deep-seated resentment.

jokes.text: I promise to enjoy the 60 minutes I'll spend having my deepest tissues palpated
jokes.setup: Johnny wanting Moira to relax at the spa
jokes.punchline: Moira's overly clinical description of a massage
jokes.explanation: Moira's tendency to use unnecessarily formal/clinical language for simple things

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: A massage is a series of movements performed on the body using pressure and friction. The practitioner will apply varying degrees of force to manipulate the soft tissues, which may result in temporary discomfort, followed by what some describe as relief. It's essentially consensual assault with essential oils.

jokes.text: Factor in five minutes for you to use the facilities and we'll be back home by noon
jokes.setup: Moira planning their spa day
jokes.punchline: Her overly detailed time management including Johnny's bathroom time
jokes.explanation: Moira's inability to just relax, even planning bathroom breaks into the schedule

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Now, I've allocated 40 minutes for my Swedish massage, 30 minutes for the seaweed wrap, 20 minutes for the facial, and Johnny, I've blocked off an hour and a half for you in the bathroom.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: An hour and a half?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, I've factored in time for you to stand there looking confused by all the products.

jokes.text: Similar texture but falls apart in emotional complexity
jokes.setup: Moira comparing fabric choices for a show
jokes.punchline: Analyzing fabric in terms of 'emotional complexity'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Moira's theatrical pretension - applying dramatic analysis to upholstery

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: This velvet has real emotional complexity. It's vulnerable, yet unapologetically sensual. This silk, on the other hand, is emotionally distant—it's beautiful, but it doesn't care if you love it.

jokes.text: It's the circle of life, John. You've just provided a nice Passover Seder for a family of hawks.
jokes.setup: Johnny worried about hitting an animal
jokes.punchline: Moira's casual, poetic dismissal mixing nature documentaries with Jewish holidays
jokes.explanation: Brilliant mix of nature documentary language with Jewish tradition - classic Moira wordplay

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, if you hit it, it's probably just returning to the circle of life. It's Passover for the animals.

jokes.text: what kind of ailurophobe sends their kitty out to play on such a busy stretch of highway?
jokes.setup: Finding out the cat has an owner
jokes.punchline: Using 'ailurophobe' (cat-hater) incorrectly while trying to sound smart
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira malapropism - using a fancy word incorrectly while trying to sound intellectual

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I for one am not about to be made a fool of by some ailurophobe who thinks they can just waltz into our lives and claim ownership of our cat.

jokes.text: Okay, like a serial killer?
jokes.setup: David arranging products at the flea market
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific comparison to serial killer organization
jokes.explanation: David's dark pop culture references applied to mundane situations - classic character trait

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm organizing these by color, size, and provenance. It's very important to have a system. Serial killers have systems, and look how organized they are.

jokes.text: If this isn't a full circle moment, then I'm not twice divorced.
jokes.setup: Wendy seeing David at the flea market
jokes.punchline: Using her multiple divorces as a measure of certainty
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor that efficiently establishes character backstory

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: David? David Rose? Oh my God, it IS you! What are you doing here?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Hello, Wendy. I'm just browsing.

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: Well, I am absolutely certain I saw you here. I'm as certain of that as I am of my marriages ending.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Which is...?

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: Four times certain.

jokes.text: Well, you know what they say about love, it's like target practice, if you just keep shooting at it...
jokes.setup: Wendy starting what sounds like wisdom about love
jokes.punchline: The metaphor completely falls apart and makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anti-wisdom - sounds profound but is complete nonsense, perfect for Wendy's character

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: Love is like a bird...

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: ...you have to feed it, or it dies. But also, you can't keep it in a cage, or it will suffocate. But if you let it out, it might fly into a window and die anyway. So really, the bird is doomed no matter what you do, which is why I don't have pets.

jokes.text: so we're reopening the Blouse Barn?
jokes.setup: Wendy explaining her booth selling general store items
jokes.punchline: David's dry observation about the similarity to her old store
jokes.explanation: David's dry wit pointing out the obvious pattern in Wendy's business ventures

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: So it's exactly like your old store, just with more desperation and a folding table.

jokes.text: Well, isn't that a compliment. For him.
jokes.setup: Wendy saying Antonio reminds her of David
jokes.punchline: David's backhanded way of taking credit while insulting Antonio
jokes.explanation: Perfect David - manages to be both self-aggrandizing and dismissive in one breath

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, Antonio does have a certain... je ne sais quoi. Though I'm not sure if it's the same je ne sais quoi that I have. Mine is more of a refined, understated elegance, whereas his seems to be more of a... how do I put this delicately... aggressive desperation.

jokes.text: Karen's a new one.
jokes.setup: Wendy calling Stevie 'Karen' instead of her actual name
jokes.punchline: Stevie's dry acknowledgment of being misnamed again
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan - acknowledging absurdity without getting worked up about it

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: It's Stevie.

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: Right, Karen.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Mm-hmm.

jokes.text: she was looking for a Jon Gosselin type
jokes.setup: David explaining Wendy's terrible taste in men
jokes.punchline: The specific reference to reality TV dad Jon Gosselin as a desirable type
jokes.explanation: Specific pop culture reference that perfectly encapsulates 'worst possible romantic choice'

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: She has terrible taste in men. She once told me she thought Jon Gosselin was attractive.

jokes.text: But I didn't pay for that. / I know, I got it at the flea market.
jokes.setup: Alexis acting like Ted is delivering a package
jokes.punchline: The reveal that he bought her a gift, not making a delivery
jokes.explanation: Sweet misdirection that sets up their roleplay scenario

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is this a package for me? I'm not expecting anything. Did I order something and black out?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: No, no. I'm not here as a delivery person. I actually... I bought you something.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait, you bought me a gift? Ted, that's so sweet!

jokes.text: Thank you for the delivery. Do you need my signature? Oops.
jokes.setup: Alexis pretends Ted is a delivery man for their roleplay
jokes.punchline: Her 'accidental' wardrobe malfunction
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy setup for their roleplay scenario

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you're here with my package?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Yeah, I have your delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, come in, come in. I wasn't expecting you so soon.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I aim to please.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh!

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Your robe—

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I'm so embarrassed. I just got out of the shower. Don't look at me!

jokes.text: seems that the strap has fallen off of your shoulder. I'd offer to put it back for you, but as a delivery guy, I don't know if it's my place.
jokes.setup: Ted and Alexis doing delivery guy roleplay
jokes.punchline: Ted's awkward, overly formal approach to sexy roleplay
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe - Ted's awkward formality in what should be a sexy moment

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Hello, I am here to deliver your package. I have filled out all the requisite forms in triplicate and I have my delivery confirmation device right here. Should I place it on the counter or would you prefer I leave it by the door? I should note that according to subsection 4.2 of the delivery company handbook, I am required to obtain a signature.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Ted, what are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I'm being sexy. Is it not working?

jokes.text: my... grandmother passed this morning. / Ew! Oh my God, Ted! / Sorry, I wasn't prepared for an improv.
jokes.setup: Ted trying to explain being 'late' for delivery
jokes.punchline: The awkward realization he brought real tragedy into sexy roleplay
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe moment - mixing real death with sexy roleplay creates perfect uncomfortable comedy

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I was late because my dog died that morning.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Ted, I'm so sorry.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, you didn't seem very sorry when you were handcuffing me to the bed saying 'Daddy's here to punish the naughty delivery boy.'

jokes.text: So, you and I just innocently witnessed an 18-wheeler barreling down...
jokes.setup: Johnny wanting to be honest about the accident
jokes.punchline: Moira immediately jumping to elaborate fictional scenarios
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - unable to just tell the truth, immediately creating drama

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I think we should just be honest about what happened with the accident.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Honest? Johnny, honesty is a luxury for people with good insurance and strong cheekbones. We were hit by a vehicle piloted by a woman named Brenda who was distracted by her emotional support llama.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: The llama had escaped from a traveling circus, and Brenda was a former trapeze artist trying to recapture her glory days. She didn't even have a license—she was operating the vehicle illegally as part of an underground car-sharing operation run by her estranged mother.

jokes.text: My husband struck down your feline with our car.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to delicately explain what happened
jokes.punchline: Moira's blunt, overly formal announcement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira - combining theatrical formality with brutal honesty

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I have been informed that my husband has engaged in sexual relations with our son's former paramour.

jokes.text: No, my husband has what's called a feather foot.
jokes.setup: Farmer blaming city people for driving too fast
jokes.punchline: Moira's made-up fancy term for gentle driving
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira invention - 'feather foot' sounds real but is completely made up

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I've been practicing what I call 'vehicular decorum' — it's a very delicate balance of grace and restraint while operating a motor vehicle.

jokes.text: The Hammam Spas are notoriously fickle about refunds.
jokes.setup: Trying to leave after telling the farmer about his cat
jokes.punchline: Moira's absurd priorities and made-up spa expertise
jokes.explanation: Moira's self-absorbed priorities - worried about spa refunds while dealing with dead cat

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I would love to stay and chat, but I have a very important appointment at the spa. I'm getting a seaweed wrap, followed by a hot stone massage, and then I'm having my chakras aligned by a shaman who only works on Tuesdays.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Plus, I promised myself I'd be home in time to practice my interpretive dance routine. It's very demanding work, and quite frankly, your cat situation, while tragic, is simply not as pressing as my wellness schedule.

jokes.text: Hm, John, shall I pick you up on the way back? I know how desperate you were for me to have my day of detox.
jokes.setup: Farmer insisting they come inside to tell his daughter
jokes.punchline: Moira trying to abandon Johnny to go to the spa alone
jokes.explanation: Moira's selfishness disguised as consideration for Johnny's wishes

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I suppose we could pop in for a moment. Johnny, darling, why don't you stay here and... chat with the livestock.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Moira, what are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm going to the spa. Alone. Without you.

jokes.text: What the actual fuck?! / They've ripped off our entire store.
jokes.setup: David seeing the knockoff store setup
jokes.punchline: His explosive realization and Stevie's calm confirmation
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between David's explosion and Stevie's deadpan matter-of-factness

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Wait. Is this... are you selling... knockoffs?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You're selling counterfeit goods out of my store?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Well, technically it's our store now.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That is not the point! This is illegal!

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: Tulips instead of roses? Woof!
jokes.setup: Examining the knockoff products
jokes.punchline: David's snobbish dismissal of the flower choice
jokes.explanation: David's design snobbery - treating flower choice as a serious aesthetic failure

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: A sunflower? Really? That's what we're going with? Sunflowers are so... cheerful. And common. They're the participation trophy of flowers.

jokes.text: That's not me! And he's still standing there!
jokes.setup: Roland confused about which David he talked to
jokes.punchline: David pointing out his doppelganger is literally right there
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of mistaken identity with David's exasperation

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Wait, which David did I talk to?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: The one that's literally standing right here.

jokes.text: You were a lot nicer when I was talking to you over there.
jokes.setup: David being rude about the knockoff products
jokes.punchline: Roland's innocent observation about the personality difference
jokes.explanation: Roland's oblivious observation highlighting David's rudeness vs his doppelganger's niceness

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: These are just knockoff versions of the real thing. They're cheap imitations that don't even come close to the quality.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You know, it's funny - you and your products are more alike than you think.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What is that supposed to mean?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, you're both kind of fake too.

jokes.text: How far do you think we'd get if we just started running?
jokes.setup: The complete disaster of the flea market situation
jokes.punchline: David's consideration of just fleeing the scene
jokes.explanation: Perfect David response to stress - flight over fight, delivered with genuine consideration

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm just going to leave. I'm going to get in the car and I'm going to drive away from here and never come back.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: And I'm going to change my name, and I'm going to start a new life somewhere where nobody knows who I am or what happened at this flea market.

jokes.text: She's either up, or takin' a leak. / Either way, great progress for Bebe.
jokes.setup: Farmer's crude description of his daughter's status
jokes.punchline: Moira's inappropriately encouraging response
jokes.explanation: Moira treating basic bodily functions as developmental milestones - perfectly awkward

dialogue.speaker: Farmer
dialogue.line: My daughter's out there somewhere, probably knee-deep in some guy.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, good for her! A girl's got to get her steps in.

jokes.text: Yes, it seems he had taken ill, and stumbled out on the road poor thing, presumably to end it all.
jokes.setup: Having to explain how the cat died
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic narrative about cat suicide
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Moira creating an elaborate suicide narrative for a cat

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, it was a Tuesday. Or a Wednesday. Time moves differently when you're grieving. I found Mr. Whiskers on the chaise lounge in the sunroom, perfectly posed, as if he'd orchestrated the whole thing. And perhaps he had. Perhaps he'd simply decided that this mortal coil was no longer worth the effort. It was very Schopenhauer of him, really. A philosophical statement wrapped in fur.

jokes.text: It smells like pennies and burps!
jokes.setup: David smelling the fake foot cream
jokes.punchline: His disgusted, specific description
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and gross description - perfectly captures David's disgust

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Is that... feet? Why does it smell like a foot had a foot?

jokes.text: Gel Time Moist Liquid. Contains 100% moist liquid.
jokes.setup: Reading the product name
jokes.punchline: The redundant, nonsensical product description
jokes.explanation: Absurd product naming - '100% moist liquid' is hilariously redundant

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's a luxury face cream that costs $800 a jar, and the description says 'luxurious luxury for your luxurious face.'

jokes.text: Uh-oh, Davey got busted!
jokes.setup: David discovering the cheap knockoff products
jokes.punchline: Roland's childish glee at David's embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Classic Roland - delighting in others' misfortune with childish language

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: These are knockoff products. This is a knockoff of my brand.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, yeah, but they're a quarter of the price!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Roland, they say 'Schitt's Creek Apothecary' on them. That's MY name.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I know! Isn't it great? I'm gonna buy a whole case!

jokes.text: pharmacies hand those out for free on Halloween.
jokes.setup: Roland pointing out how cheap the products are
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of pharmacies giving out beauty products for Halloween
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd image that somehow feels plausible - Halloween beauty products as treats

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You know, these products are so cheap, pharmacies are gonna start giving them out for Halloween instead of candy.

jokes.text: You pulled some new tricks out. / New to you.
jokes.setup: Ted complimenting Alexis on their intimate encounter
jokes.punchline: Alexis's matter-of-fact correction about her experience level
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis - casually revealing her extensive experience without embarrassment

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: That was... wow. I mean, you really knew what you were doing.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, no I didn't. I was just lying there thinking about my highlights the whole time.

jokes.text: the door was unlocked the entire time! / They would've gotten quite the show!
jokes.setup: Alexis realizing they could have been caught
jokes.punchline: Ted's enthusiastic response about being watched
jokes.explanation: Ted's unexpected enthusiasm for exhibitionism contrasts with his usual awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, we could have been caught!

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Yeah, that would have been so hot.

jokes.text: How are we still trapped in this gothic horror show?
jokes.setup: Being stuck at the farmhouse longer than expected
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic characterization of a simple farmhouse visit
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - turning a mundane situation into literary drama

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I came to this godforsaken farmhouse for what I thought would be a quaint weekend respite, and now I'm being held prisoner like some sort of political hostage in a third-world country!

jokes.text: The funeral... for your cat?
jokes.setup: Farmer demanding they pay for funeral expenses
jokes.punchline: Johnny's incredulous realization about a cat funeral
jokes.explanation: Johnny's reasonable confusion about the absurd request for cat funeral expenses

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. You're telling me you want me to pay for a cat's funeral?

dialogue.speaker: Farmer
dialogue.line: That's right. Fluffy was a good mouser.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: A good mouser? The cat is dead! What is it mousing now, the afterlife?

jokes.text: Perhaps it's the lack of air, but I believe I saw three or four cats running around since this interrogation began.
jokes.setup: The claim that this was the daughter's 'only' cat
jokes.punchline: Moira's dry observation about the obvious lie while blaming oxygen deprivation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira - calling out obvious lies with theatrical flair and medical excuse

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, that's clearly not true. You've had multiple cats. I assume the oxygen deprivation from living in that car has affected your memory.

jokes.text: No! She likes the Kinky Shoes. / I believe it's Kinky Boots.
jokes.setup: Baby wanting tickets to a Broadway show
jokes.punchline: Moira's automatic correction of the show title
jokes.explanation: Moira can't help but correct theater terminology even in this stressful situation

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I want tickets to Hamilton.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You mean Hamiltrton?

jokes.text: I'm her mother! / Yes, well that... / Yes, you are!
jokes.setup: Johnny suggesting Baby take her grandmother to the spa
jokes.punchline: The awkward realization that this elderly-looking woman is the mother
jokes.explanation: Awkward age confusion moment with Johnny's fumbling recovery

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Why don't you take your grandmother to the spa?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That's my mother.

jokes.text: Who's Brad? / My fiance. / I thought you and Antonio were... / Oh David... We're business partners.
jokes.setup: David's confusion about Wendy's relationships
jokes.punchline: The revelation that there are even more men involved than David thought
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of confusion about Wendy's complicated romantic/business life

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Wait, so there's Marcus, and then there's... who else?

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: Well, there's Marcus, and then there's Derek.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Derek. Right. And that's...

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: And then there's Steve.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Steve. Okay. So Marcus, Derek, and Steve.

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: And Gary.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: There's a Gary?

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: And Brian.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: How many men are there?

jokes.text: Who's Brad? My fiance. I thought you and Antonio were... Oh David... We're business partners.
jokes.setup: David's assumption about Wendy and Antonio's relationship
jokes.punchline: Revealing David completely misunderstood the situation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection revealing David's wrong assumptions

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: So you and Antonio are... together?

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: What? No. He's my realtor.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Your realtor.

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I thought you two were... I mean, the way you were talking about him...

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: David, he sold me a condo.

jokes.text: they had to put the mirror on the ceiling.
jokes.setup: Wendy describing how crowded Brad and Antonio's apartment is
jokes.punchline: The innocent explanation for what sounds like a sexual setup
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection - sounds sexual but is actually about space constraints

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: Their apartment is so small, when Brad and Antonio have people over, someone always ends up in the bedroom.

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: Last week, I was in there with three other people, and we were just... packed in there together.

jokes.text: what would I do without you? / I literally don't know, Wendy.
jokes.setup: Wendy expressing gratitude to David
jokes.punchline: David's matter-of-fact agreement about her helplessness
jokes.explanation: Perfect David response - taking credit while being brutally honest about Wendy's incompetence

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: I'm so grateful for everything you do for me, David. I don't know what I'd do without you.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, well, you'd probably be lost. Literally and figuratively.

jokes.text: A few years ago. And it just been real tough to get over.
jokes.setup: Ted's earlier lie about his grandmother dying
jokes.punchline: His attempt to maintain the fiction with more elaborate details
jokes.explanation: Ted doubling down on his earlier improvised lie with awkward additional details

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: So my grandmother, she actually died twice.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: The first time was in 1987, but then she got better.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: And then the second time, it really stuck.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Very sudden. She was doing yoga.

jokes.text: And if we're pointing fingers, David, I've done it on a lot of sinks and I've never had an issue.
jokes.setup: David blaming them for breaking the sink
jokes.punchline: Alexis's matter-of-fact defense of her sink-related activities
jokes.explanation: Peak Alexis - casually revealing extensive sink experience with no shame or self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I didn't break the sink, David. I was just washing my hair in it because the shower was taking too long, and then I may have accidentally kicked it, but that was after I was done washing my hair, so technically the sink was already broken by the time I kicked it.

jokes.text: When you say you've done it on a lot of sinks...
jokes.setup: Alexis's casual admission about sink activities
jokes.punchline: David's horrified attempt to process this information
jokes.explanation: David's brotherly horror at learning too much about Alexis's activities - perfect sibling dynamic

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: What? I wash my face in the sink sometimes.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Sometimes? Alexis, that is a sink. That is not a bathing facility.

tmdb_overview: Johnny and Moira’s afternoon plans are interrupted when they accidentally hit a cat. David runs into an old colleague at the flea market whose new business bears a striking resemblance to Rose Apothecary.
insight: Character escalation drives a quieter episode as the Rose family navigates social humiliation.
